017package org.apache.juneau.marshall;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.ClassMeta.Category.*;
024
025import java.io.*;
026import java.lang.annotation.*;
027import java.lang.reflect.*;
028import java.lang.reflect.Proxy;
029import java.net.*;
030import java.time.*;
031import java.time.temporal.*;
032import java.util.*;
033import java.util.function.*;
034import java.util.stream.*;
035
036import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.commons.conversion.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
045import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
046
047/**
048 * A wrapper class around the {@link Class} object that provides cached information about that class.
049 *
050 * <p>
051 * Instances of this class can be created through the {@link MarshallingContext#getClassMeta(Class)} method.
052 *
053 * <p>
054 * The {@link MarshallingContext} class will cache and reuse instances of this class except for the following class types:
055 * <ul>
056 *    <li>Arrays
057 *    <li>Maps with non-Object key/values.
058 *    <li>Collections with non-Object key/values.
059 * </ul>
060 *
061 * <p>
062 * This class is tied to the {@link MarshallingContext} class because it's that class that makes the determination of what is
063 * a bean.
064 *
065 *
066 * @param <T> The class type of the wrapped class.
067 */
068@BeanType(properties = "innerClass,elementType,keyType,valueType,notABeanReason,initException,beanMeta")
069@SuppressWarnings({
070   "java:S1200",  // Class has 23 dependencies, acceptable for this core reflection metadata class
071   "java:S1452",  // Wildcard required - ClassMeta<?>, ObjectSwap<T,?>, etc. for element/component types
072   "java:S6539"   // Monster Class: ClassMeta is a focused reflection-metadata cache; splitting would increase coupling
073})
074public final class ClassMeta<T> extends BeanInfo<T> {
075
076   private static class Categories {
077      int bits;
078
079      public boolean same(Categories cat) {
080         return cat.bits == bits;
081      }
082
083      boolean is(Category c) {
084         return (bits & c.mask) != 0;
085      }
086
087      boolean isUnknown() {
088         return bits == 0;
089      }
090
091      Categories set(Category c) {
092         bits |= c.mask;
093         return this;
094      }
095   }
096
097   enum Category {
098      MAP(0),
099      COLLECTION(1),
100      NUMBER(2),
101      DECIMAL(3),
102      DATE(4),
103      ARRAY(5),
104      ENUM(6),
105      CHARSEQ(8),
106      STR(9),
107      URI(10),
108      BEANMAP(11),
109      READER(12),
110      INPUTSTREAM(13),
111      ARGS(14),
112      CALENDAR(15),
113      TEMPORAL(16),
114      LIST(17),
115      SET(18),
116      DELEGATE(19),
117      BEAN(20),
118      ITERATOR(21),
119      ITERABLE(22),
120      STREAM(23),
121      DURATION(24),
122      PERIOD(25);
123
124      private final int mask;
125
126      Category(int bitPosition) {
127         this.mask = 1 << bitPosition;
128      }
129   }
130
131   /**
132    * Generated classes shouldn't be cacheable to prevent needlessly filling up the cache.
133    */
134   private static boolean isCacheable(Class<?> c) {
135      var n = cn(c);
136      var x = n.charAt(n.length() - 1);  // All generated classes appear to end with digits.
137      return !(x >= '0' && x <= '9' && (n.indexOf("$$") != -1 || n.startsWith("sun") || n.startsWith("com.sun") || n.indexOf("$Proxy") != -1));
138   }
139
140   private final List<ClassMeta<?>> args;                                     // Arg types if this is an array of args.
141   private final MarshallingContext marshallingContext;                                     // The bean context that created this object.
142   private final Supplier<BuilderSwap<T,?>> builderSwap;                      // The builder swap associated with this bean (if it has one).
143   private final Categories cat;                                              // The class category.
144   private final Cache<Class<?>,ObjectSwap<?,?>> childSwapMap;                // Maps normal subclasses to ObjectSwaps.
145   private final Supplier<List<ObjectSwap<?,?>>> childSwaps;                  // Any ObjectSwaps where the normal type is a subclass of this class.
146   private final Cache<Class<?>,ObjectSwap<?,?>> childUnswapMap;              // Maps swap subclasses to ObjectSwaps.
147   private final Supplier<String> beanDictionaryName;                             // The dictionary name of this class if it has one.
148   private final Supplier<ClassMeta<?>> elementType;                          // If ARRAY or COLLECTION, the element class type.
149   private final NullableSupplier<String> example;                            // Example JSON.
150   private final NullableSupplier<FieldInfo> exampleField;                    // The @Example-annotated field (if it has one).
151   private final NullableSupplier<MethodInfo> exampleMethod;                  // The example() or @Example-annotated method (if it has one).
152   private final Supplier<BidiMap<Object,String>> enumValues;
153   private final NullableSupplier<MethodInfo> fromStringMethod;               // Static fromString(String) or equivalent method
154   private final NullableSupplier<ClassInfoTyped<? extends T>> implClass;     // The implementation class to use if this is an interface.
155   private final Supplier<KeyValueTypes> keyValueTypes;                        // Key and value types for MAP types.
156   private final Supplier<Type[]> parameters;                                  // Type parameters for converter dispatch.
157   private final NullableSupplier<MarshalledFilter> marshalledFilter;
158   private final Supplier<Property<T,Object>> nameProperty;                   // The method to set the name on an object (if it has one).
159   private final NullableSupplier<ConstructorInfo> noArgConstructor;          // The no-arg constructor for this class (if it has one).
160   private final Supplier<Property<T,Object>> parentProperty;                 // The method to set the parent on an object (if it has one).
161   private final Cache<String,Optional<?>> properties;
162   private final NullableSupplier<ConstructorInfo> stringConstructor;         // The X(String) constructor (if it has one).
163   private final Supplier<List<ObjectSwap<T,?>>> swaps;                       // The object POJO swaps associated with this bean (if it has any).
164   private final NullableSupplier<org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.BeanMeta.BeanMetaValue<T>> beanMeta;
165
166   private record KeyValueTypes(ClassMeta<?> keyType, ClassMeta<?> valueType) {
167      Optional<ClassMeta<?>> optKeyType() { return opt(keyType()); }
168      Optional<ClassMeta<?>> optValueType() { return opt(valueType()); }
169   }
170
171   /**
172    * Construct a new {@code ClassMeta} based on the specified {@link Class}.
173    *
174    * @param innerClass The class being wrapped.
175    * @param marshallingContext The bean context that created this object.
176    * @param delayedInit
177    *    Don't call init() in constructor.
178    *    Used for delayed initialization when the possibility of class reference loops exist.
179    */
180   @SuppressWarnings({
181      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for ClassMeta initialization with category detection
182   })
183   ClassMeta(Class<T> innerClass, MarshallingContext marshallingContext) {
184      super(innerClass);
185      this.marshallingContext = marshallingContext;
186      this.cat = new Categories();
187
188      // We always immediately add this class meta to the bean context cache so that we can resolve recursive references.
189      if (nn(marshallingContext) && nn(marshallingContext.getCmCache()) && isCacheable(innerClass))
190         marshallingContext.getCmCache().put(innerClass, this);
191
192      var ap = marshallingContext.getAnnotationProvider();
193
194      if (isAssignableTo(Delegate.class)) {
195         cat.set(DELEGATE);
196      }
197      if (isEnum()) {
198         cat.set(ENUM);
199      } else if (isAssignableTo(CharSequence.class)) {
200         cat.set(CHARSEQ);
201         if (is(String.class)) {
202            cat.set(STR);
203         }
204      } else if (isAssignableTo(Number.class) || isAny(byte.class, short.class, int.class, long.class, float.class, double.class)) {
205         cat.set(NUMBER);
206         if (isAssignableToAny(Float.class, Double.class) || isAny(float.class, double.class)) {
207            cat.set(DECIMAL);
208         }
209      } else if (isAssignableTo(Collection.class)) {
210         cat.set(COLLECTION);
211         if (isAssignableTo(Set.class)) {
212            cat.set(SET);
213         } else if (isAssignableTo(List.class)) {
214            cat.set(LIST);
215         }
216      } else if (isAssignableTo(Iterable.class)) {
217         cat.set(ITERABLE);
218      } else if (isAssignableTo(Iterator.class) || isAssignableTo(Enumeration.class)) {
219         cat.set(ITERATOR);
220      } else if (isAssignableTo(BaseStream.class)) {
221         cat.set(STREAM);
222      } else if (isAssignableTo(Map.class)) {
223         cat.set(MAP);
224         if (isAssignableTo(BeanMap.class)) {
225            cat.set(BEANMAP);
226         }
227      } else if (isAssignableToAny(Date.class, Calendar.class)) {
228         if (isAssignableTo(Date.class)) {
229            cat.set(DATE);
230         } else if (isAssignableTo(Calendar.class)) {
231            cat.set(CALENDAR);
232         }
233      } else if (is(Duration.class)) {
234         cat.set(DURATION);
235      } else if (is(Period.class)) {
236         cat.set(PERIOD);
237      } else if (isAssignableTo(Temporal.class)) {
238         cat.set(TEMPORAL);
239      } else if (isAssignableTo(javax.xml.datatype.XMLGregorianCalendar.class)) {
240         cat.set(CALENDAR);
241      } else if (inner().isArray()) {
242         cat.set(ARRAY);
243      } else if (isAssignableToAny(URL.class, URI.class) || ap.has(Uri.class, this)) {
244         cat.set(URI);
245      } else if (isAssignableTo(Reader.class)) {
246         cat.set(READER);
247      } else if (isAssignableTo(InputStream.class)) {
248         cat.set(INPUTSTREAM);
249      }
250
251      beanMeta = memoize(this::findBeanMeta);
252      builderSwap = memoize(this::findBuilderSwap);
253      childSwapMap = Cache.<Class<?>,ObjectSwap<?,?>>create().supplier(this::findSwap).build();
254      childSwaps = memoize(this::findChildSwaps);
255      childUnswapMap = Cache.<Class<?>,ObjectSwap<?,?>>create().supplier(this::findUnswap).build();
256      beanDictionaryName = memoize(this::findBeanDictionaryName);
257      elementType = memoize(this::findElementType);
258      enumValues = memoize(this::findEnumValues);
259      example = memoize(this::findExample);
260      exampleField = memoize(this::findExampleField);
261      exampleMethod = memoize(this::findExampleMethod);
262      fromStringMethod = memoize(this::findFromStringMethod);
263      implClass = memoize(this::findImplClass);
264      keyValueTypes = memoize(this::findKeyValueTypes);
265      parameters = memoize(this::findParameters);
266      marshalledFilter = memoize(this::findMarshalledFilter);
267      nameProperty = memoize(this::findNameProperty);
268      noArgConstructor = memoize(this::findNoArgConstructor);
269      parentProperty = memoize(this::findParentProperty);
270      properties = Cache.<String,Optional<?>>create().build();
271      stringConstructor = memoize(this::findStringConstructor);
272      swaps = memoize(this::findSwaps);
273
274      this.args = null;
275   }
276
277   protected ObjectSwap<?,?> findSwap(Class<?> c) {
278      return childSwaps.get().stream().filter(x -> x.getNormalClass().isAssignableFrom(c)).findFirst().orElse(null);
279   }
280
281   protected ObjectSwap<?,?> findUnswap(Class<?> c) {
282      return childSwaps.get().stream().filter(x -> x.getSwapClass().isAssignableFrom(c)).findFirst().orElse(null);
283   }
284
285
286   /**
287    * Constructor for args-arrays.
288    */
289   @SuppressWarnings({
290      "unchecked" // Object[] cast required for args-array type handling
291   })
292   ClassMeta(List<ClassMeta<?>> args) {
293      super((Class<T>)Object[].class);
294      this.args = args;
295      this.childSwaps = memoize(this::findChildSwaps);
296      this.childSwapMap = null;
297      this.childUnswapMap = null;
298      this.cat = new Categories().set(ARGS);
299      this.marshallingContext = null;
300      this.elementType = memoize(this::findElementType);
301      this.keyValueTypes = memoize(this::findKeyValueTypes);
302      this.beanMeta = memoize(this::findBeanMeta);
303      this.swaps = memoize(this::findSwaps);
304      this.fromStringMethod = memoize(this::findFromStringMethod);
305      this.exampleMethod = memoize(this::findExampleMethod);
306      this.parentProperty = memoize(this::findParentProperty);
307      this.nameProperty = memoize(this::findNameProperty);
308      this.exampleField = memoize(this::findExampleField);
309      this.noArgConstructor = memoize(this::findNoArgConstructor);
310      this.properties = Cache.<String,Optional<?>>create().build();
311      this.stringConstructor = memoize(this::findStringConstructor);
312      this.marshalledFilter = memoize(this::findMarshalledFilter);
313      this.builderSwap = memoize(this::findBuilderSwap);
314      this.example = memoize(this::findExample);
315      this.implClass = memoize(this::findImplClass);
316      this.enumValues = memoize(this::findEnumValues);
317      this.beanDictionaryName = memoize(this::findBeanDictionaryName);
318      this.parameters = memoize(this::findParameters);
319   }
320
321   /**
322    * Copy constructor.
323    *
324    * <p>
325    * Used for creating Map and Collection class metas that shouldn't be cached.
326    */
327   ClassMeta(ClassMeta<T> mainType, ClassMeta<?> keyType, ClassMeta<?> valueType, ClassMeta<?> elementType) {
328      super(mainType.inner());
329      this.childSwaps = mainType.childSwaps;
330      this.childSwapMap = mainType.childSwapMap;
331      this.childUnswapMap = mainType.childUnswapMap;
332      this.cat = mainType.cat;
333      this.fromStringMethod = mainType.fromStringMethod;
334      this.marshallingContext = mainType.marshallingContext;
335      this.elementType = elementType != null ? memoize(()->elementType) : mainType.elementType;
336      this.keyValueTypes = (keyType != null || valueType != null) ? memoize(()->new KeyValueTypes(keyType, valueType)) : mainType.keyValueTypes;
337      this.beanMeta = mainType.beanMeta;
338      this.swaps = mainType.swaps;
339      this.exampleMethod = mainType.exampleMethod;
340      this.args = null;
341      this.parentProperty = mainType.parentProperty;
342      this.nameProperty = mainType.nameProperty;
343      this.exampleField = mainType.exampleField;
344      this.noArgConstructor = mainType.noArgConstructor;
345      this.properties = mainType.properties;
346      this.stringConstructor = mainType.stringConstructor;
347      this.marshalledFilter = mainType.marshalledFilter;
348      this.builderSwap = mainType.builderSwap;
349      this.example = mainType.example;
350      this.implClass = mainType.implClass;
351      this.enumValues = mainType.enumValues;
352      this.beanDictionaryName = mainType.beanDictionaryName;
353      this.parameters = memoize(this::findParameters);
354   }
355
356   /**
357    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class can be instantiated as a bean.
358    * Returns <jk>false</jk> if this is a non-static member class and the outer object does not match the class type of
359    * the defining class.
360    *
361    * @param outer
362    *    The outer class object for non-static member classes.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for non-member or static classes.
363    * @return
364    *    <jk>true</jk> if a new instance of this bean can be created within the context of the specified outer object.
365    */
366   public boolean canCreateNewBean(Object outer) {
367      var bm = getBeanMeta();
368      if (bm == null || ! bm.hasConstructor())
369         return false;
370      if (isMemberClass() && isNotStatic())
371         return nn(outer) && bm.getConstructor().hasParameterTypes(outer.getClass());
372      return true;
373   }
374
375   /**
376    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class has a no-arg constructor or invocation handler.
377    *
378    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a new instance of this class can be constructed.
379    */
380   @Override
381   public boolean canCreateNewInstance() {
382      if (isMemberClass() && isNotStatic())
383         return false;
384      var bm = getBeanMeta();
385      return noArgConstructor.isPresent() || (bm != null && bm.getBeanProxyInvocationHandler() != null) || (isArray() && elementType.get().canCreateNewInstance());
386   }
387
388   /**
389    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class has a no-arg constructor or invocation handler.
390    * Returns <jk>false</jk> if this is a non-static member class and the outer object does not match the class type of
391    * the defining class.
392    *
393    * @param outer
394    *    The outer class object for non-static member classes.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for non-member or static classes.
395    * @return
396    *    <jk>true</jk> if a new instance of this class can be created within the context of the specified outer object.
397    */
398   @Override
399   public boolean canCreateNewInstance(Object outer) {
400      if (isMemberClass() && isNotStatic())
401         return nn(outer) && noArgConstructor.map(x -> x.hasParameterTypes(outer.getClass())).orElse(false);
402      return canCreateNewInstance();
403   }
404
405   /**
406    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class can call the {@link #newInstanceFromString(Object, String)} method.
407    *
408    * @param outer
409    *    The outer class object for non-static member classes.
410    *    Can be <jk>null</jk> for non-member or static classes.
411    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class has a no-arg constructor or invocation handler.
412    */
413   public boolean canCreateNewInstanceFromString(Object outer) {
414      if (fromStringMethod.isPresent())
415         return true;
416      if (stringConstructor.isPresent()) {
417         if (isMemberClass() && isNotStatic())
418            return nn(outer) && stringConstructor.map(x -> x.hasParameterTypes(outer.getClass(), String.class)).orElse(false);
419         return true;
420      }
421      return false;
422   }
423
424   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
425   public boolean equals(Object o) {
426      return (o instanceof ClassMeta<?>) && super.equals(o);
427   }
428
429   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
430   public int hashCode() {
431      return super.hashCode();
432   }
433
434   /**
435    * Performs an action on all matching annotations of the specified type defined on this class or parent classes/interfaces in parent-to-child order.
436    *
437    * @param <A> The annotation type to look for.
438    * @param type The annotation to search for.
439    * @param filter A predicate to apply to the entries to determine if action should be performed.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
440    * @param action An action to perform on the entry.
441    * @return This object.
442    */
443   public <A extends Annotation> ClassMeta<T> forEachAnnotation(Class<A> type, Predicate<A> filter, Consumer<A> action) {
444      if (marshallingContext != null) {
445         marshallingContext.getAnnotationProvider().find(type, this).stream().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).filter(x -> filter == null || filter.test(x)).forEach(action::accept);
446      }
447      return this;
448   }
449
450   /**
451    * Returns the argument metadata at the specified index if this is an args metadata object.
452    *
453    * @param index The argument index.
454    * @return The The argument metadata.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
455    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If this metadata object is not a list of arguments, or the index is out of range.
456    */
457   public ClassMeta<?> getArg(int index) {
458      if (nn(args) && index >= 0 && index < args.size())
459         return args.get(index);
460      throw bex("Invalid argument index specified:  {0}.  Only {1} arguments are defined.", index, args == null ? 0 : args.size());
461   }
462
463   /**
464    * Returns the argument types of this meta.
465    *
466    * @return The argument types of this meta, or <jk>null</jk> if this isn't an array of argument types.
467    */
468   public List<ClassMeta<?>> getArgs() { return args; }
469
470   /**
471    * Returns the {@link MarshallingContext} that created this object.
472    *
473    * <p>
474    * Covariantly overrides {@link BeanInfo#getMarshallingContext()} to narrow the return type from
475    * {@link Object} (commons SPI) back to {@link MarshallingContext} (marshalling-side concrete type).
476    *
477    * @return The bean context.
478    */
479   @Override
480   public MarshallingContext getMarshallingContext() { return marshallingContext; }
481
482   /**
483    * Returns the {@link BeanConfigContext} snapshot carried by the {@link MarshallingContext} that built this
484    * {@link ClassMeta}.
485    *
486    * <p>
487    * Satisfies the {@link BeanInfo#getBeanConfigContext()} commons SPI contract.
488    *
489    * @return The bean-modeling configuration of the marshalling context.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
490    */
491   @Override
492   public BeanConfigContext getBeanConfigContext() { return marshallingContext.getBeanConfigContext(); }
493
494   /**
495    * Returns the {@link BeanMeta} associated with this class.
496    *
497    * @return
498    *    The {@link BeanMeta} associated with this class, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no bean meta associated with
499    *    this class.
500    */
501   public org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.BeanMeta<T> getBeanMeta() {
502      return beanMeta.get().beanMeta();
503   }
504
505   /**
506    * Returns the bean registry for this class.
507    *
508    * <p>
509    * This bean registry contains names specified in the {@link Marshalled#dictionary() @Marshalled(dictionary)} annotation
510    * defined on the class, regardless of whether the class is an actual bean.
511    * This allows interfaces to define subclasses with type names.
512    *
513    * <p>
514    * This is a shortcut for calling getBeanMeta().getBeanRegistry().
515    *
516    * @return The bean registry for this class, or <jk>null</jk> if no bean registry is associated with it.
517    */
518   public BeanRegistry getBeanRegistry() {
519      return beanMeta.get().optBeanMeta().map(x -> (BeanRegistry) x.getBeanRegistry()).orElse(null);
520   }
521
522   /**
523    * Returns the builder swap associated with this class.
524    *
525    * @param session The current bean session (retained for API compatibility).
526    * @return The builder swap associated with this class, or <jk>null</jk> if it doesn't exist.
527    */
528   @SuppressWarnings({
529      "java:S1172" // 'session' retained for public-API/binary compatibility; the builder swap is session-independent.
530   })
531   public BuilderSwap<T,?> getBuilderSwap(MarshallingSession session) {
532      return builderSwap.get();
533   }
534
535   /**
536    * Returns the bean dictionary name associated with this class.
537    *
538    * <p>
539    * The lexical name is defined by {@link Marshalled#typeName() @Marshalled(typeName)}.
540    *
541    * @return
542    *    The type name associated with this bean class, or <jk>null</jk> if there is no type name defined or this
543    *    isn't a bean.
544    */
545   public String getBeanDictionaryName() {
546      return beanDictionaryName.get();
547   }
548
549   /**
550    * For array and {@code Collection} types, returns the class type of the components of the array or
551    * {@code Collection}.
552    *
553    * @return The element class type, or <jk>null</jk> if this class is not an array or Collection.
554    */
555   @Override
556   public ClassMeta<?> getElementType() { return elementType.get(); }
557
558   /**
559    * Returns the example of this class.
560    *
561    * @param session
562    *    The bean session.
563    *    <br>Required because the example method may take it in as a parameter.
564    * @param jpSession The JSON parser for parsing examples into POJOs.
565    * @return The serialized class type, or this object if no swap is associated with the class.
566    */
567   @SuppressWarnings({
568      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
569      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
570   })
571   public T getExample(MarshallingSession session, JsonParserSession jpSession) {
572      try {
573         if (example.isPresent())
574            return jpSession.parse(example.get(), this);
575         if (exampleMethod.isPresent())
576            return (T)exampleMethod.get().invokeLenient(null, session);
577         if (exampleField.isPresent())
578            return (T)exampleField.get().get(null);
579
580         if (isCollection()) {
581            var etExample = getElementType().getExample(session, jpSession);
582            if (nn(etExample)) {
583               if (canCreateNewInstance()) {
584                  var c = (Collection<Object>)newInstance();
585                  c.add(etExample);
586                  return (T)c;
587               }
588               return (T)Collections.singleton(etExample);
589            }
590         } else if (super.isArray()) {
591            var etExample = getElementType().getExample(session, jpSession);
592            if (nn(etExample)) {
593               var o = Array.newInstance(getElementType().inner(), 1);
594               Array.set(o, 0, etExample);
595               return (T)o;
596            }
597         } else if (isMap()) {
598            var vtExample = getValueType().getExample(session, jpSession);
599            var ktExample = getKeyType().getExample(session, jpSession);
600            if (nn(ktExample) && nn(vtExample)) {
601               if (canCreateNewInstance()) {
602                  var m = (Map<Object,Object>)newInstance();
603                  m.put(ktExample, vtExample);
604                  return (T)m;
605               }
606               return (T)Collections.singletonMap(ktExample, vtExample);
607            }
608         }
609
610         return null;
611      } catch (Exception e) {
612         throw new ClassMetaRuntimeException(e);
613      }
614   }
615
616   /**
617    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class can be instantiated from the specified input type.
618    *
619    * @param c The input class type.
620    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a conversion exists.
621    */
622   public boolean canConvertFrom(Class<?> c) {
623      return BasicConverter.INSTANCE.canConvert(c, inner());
624   }
625
626   /**
627    * Returns the no-arg constructor for this class based on the {@link Marshalled#implClass()} value.
628    *
629    * @param conVis The constructor visibility.
630    * @return The no-arg constructor for this class, or <jk>null</jk> if it does not exist.
631    */
632   public ConstructorInfo getImplClassConstructor(Visibility conVis) {
633      return implClass.map(x -> x.getNoArgConstructor(conVis).orElse(null)).orElse(null);
634   }
635
636
637   /**
638    * For {@code Map} types, returns the class type of the keys of the {@code Map}.
639    *
640    * @return The key class type, or <jk>null</jk> if this class is not a Map.
641    */
642   @Override
643   public ClassMeta<?> getKeyType() {
644      return keyValueTypes.get().keyType();
645   }
646
647   /**
648    * Returns the method or field annotated with {@link NameProperty @NameProperty}.
649    *
650    * @return
651    *    The method or field  annotated with {@link NameProperty @NameProperty} or <jk>null</jk> if method does not
652    *    exist.
653    */
654   public Property<T,Object> getNameProperty() { return nameProperty.get(); }
655
656   /**
657    * Returns the reason why this class is not a bean, or <jk>null</jk> if it is a bean.
658    *
659    * @return The reason why this class is not a bean, or <jk>null</jk> if it is a bean.
660    */
661   public synchronized String getNotABeanReason() {
662      return beanMeta.get().notABeanReason();
663   }
664
665   /**
666    * If this is an {@link Optional}, returns an empty optional.
667    *
668    * <p>
669    * Note that if this is a nested optional, will recursively create empty optionals.
670    *
671    * @return An empty optional, or <jk>null</jk> if this isn't an optional.
672    */
673   @SuppressWarnings({
674      "java:S2789" // null intentionally signals "not an Optional type"; callers check for null
675   })
676   @Override
677   public Optional<?> getOptionalDefault() {
678      if (isPrimitiveOptional())
679         return opte();
680      if (isOptional())
681         return opt(getElementType().getOptionalDefault());
682      return null;
683   }
684
685   /**
686    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this is one of the primitive {@link Optional} cousins
687    * ({@link OptionalInt}, {@link OptionalLong}, or {@link OptionalDouble}).
688    *
689    * <p>
690    * These types carry no generic element type but are treated as first-class optional types by the
691    * marshallers (mirroring the {@link Optional} contract — empty ≡ null/absent, present unwraps to the
692    * primitive number).  See {@link #getElementType()} which synthesizes the matching boxed element type.
693    *
694    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this is {@link OptionalInt}/{@link OptionalLong}/{@link OptionalDouble}.
695    */
696   public boolean isPrimitiveOptional() {
697      return is(OptionalInt.class) || is(OptionalLong.class) || is(OptionalDouble.class);
698   }
699
700   /**
701    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Optional} or one of its primitive cousins
702    * ({@link OptionalInt}/{@link OptionalLong}/{@link OptionalDouble}).
703    *
704    * <p>
705    * Overrides {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ClassInfo#isOptional()} so the marshalling layer
706    * treats the three primitive optional types as first-class optionals (synthetic boxed element type,
707    * empty-as-null contract) without registering swaps for them.
708    *
709    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this is an optional-like type.
710    */
711   @Override
712   public boolean isOptional() {
713      return super.isOptional() || isPrimitiveOptional();
714   }
715
716   /**
717    * Returns the method or field annotated with {@link ParentProperty @ParentProperty}.
718    *
719    * @return
720    *    The method or field annotated with {@link ParentProperty @ParentProperty} or <jk>null</jk> if method does not
721    *    exist.
722    */
723   public Property<T,Object> getParentProperty() { return parentProperty.get(); }
724
725   /**
726    * Returns a lazily-computed, cached property value for this {@link ClassMeta} instance.
727    *
728    * <p>
729    * This method provides a memoization mechanism for expensive computations. The property value is computed
730    * on the first call using the provided function, then cached for subsequent calls with the same property name.
731    *
732    * <p>
733    * The function is only invoked once per property name per {@link ClassMeta} instance. Subsequent calls
734    * with the same name will return the cached value without re-invoking the function.
735    *
736    * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
737    * <p>
738    * This method is thread-safe. If multiple threads call this method simultaneously with the same property name,
739    * the function may be invoked multiple times, but only one result will be cached and returned.
740    *
741    * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
742    * <p class='bjava'>
743    *    <jc>// Compute and cache an expensive property</jc>
744    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>computedValue</jv> = classMeta.<jsm>getProperty</jsm>(<js>"expensiveProperty"</js>, cm -&gt; {
745    *       <jc>// Expensive computation that only runs once</jc>
746    *       <jk>return</jk> performExpensiveComputation(cm);
747    *    });
748    *
749    *    <jc>// Subsequent calls return cached value</jc>
750    *    Optional&lt;String&gt; <jv>cached</jv> = classMeta.<jsm>getProperty</jsm>(<js>"expensiveProperty"</js>, cm -&gt; {
751    *       <jc>// This function is NOT called again</jc>
752    *       <jk>return</jk> performExpensiveComputation(cm);
753    *    });
754    * </p>
755    *
756    * @param <T2> The type of the property value.
757    * @param name The unique name identifying this property. Used as the cache key.
758    * @param function The function that computes the property value. Receives this {@link ClassMeta} instance as input.
759    *    Only invoked if the property hasn't been computed yet. Can return <jk>null</jk>.
760    * @return An {@link Optional} containing the property value if the function returned a non-null value,
761    *    otherwise an empty {@link Optional}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
762    */
763   @SuppressWarnings({
764      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to Optional<T2>
765   })
766   public <T2> Optional<T2> getProperty(String name, Function<ClassMeta<?>,T2> function) {
767      return (Optional<T2>)properties.get(name, () -> opt(function.apply(this)));
768   }
769
770   /**
771    * Returns the interface proxy invocation handler for this class.
772    *
773    * @return The interface proxy invocation handler, or <jk>null</jk> if it does not exist.
774    */
775   public InvocationHandler getProxyInvocationHandler() {
776      return beanMeta.get().optBeanMeta().map(x -> x.getBeanProxyInvocationHandler()).orElse(null);
777   }
778
779
780   /**
781    * Returns the serialized (swapped) form of this class if there is an {@link ObjectSwap} associated with it.
782    *
783    * @param session
784    *    The bean session.
785    *    <br>Required because the swap used may depend on the media type being serialized or parsed.
786    * @return The serialized class type, or this object if no swap is associated with the class.
787    */
788   public ClassMeta<?> getSerializedClassMeta(MarshallingSession session) {
789      var ps = getSwap(session);
790      return (ps == null ? this : ps.getSwapClassMeta(session));
791   }
792
793
794   /**
795    * Returns the {@link ObjectSwap} associated with this class that's the best match for the specified session.
796    *
797    * @param session
798    *    The current bean session.
799    *    <br>If multiple swaps are associated with a class, only the first one with a matching media type will
800    *    be returned.
801    * @return
802    *    The {@link ObjectSwap} associated with this class, or <jk>null</jk> if there are no POJO swaps associated with
803    *    this class.
804    */
805   public ObjectSwap<T,?> getSwap(MarshallingSession session) {
806      var swapsList = swaps.get();
807      if (! swapsList.isEmpty()) {
808         var matchQuant = 0;
809         ObjectSwap<T,?> matchSwap = null;
810
811         for (var swap : swapsList) {
812            var q = swap.match(session);
813            if (q > matchQuant) {
814               matchQuant = q;
815               matchSwap = swap;
816            }
817         }
818
819         if (matchSwap != null)
820            return matchSwap;
821      }
822      return null;
823   }
824
825   /**
826    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class can be converted to the specified output type.
827    *
828    * @param c The output class type.
829    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a conversion exists.
830    */
831   public boolean canConvertTo(Class<?> c) {
832      return BasicConverter.INSTANCE.canConvert(inner(), c);
833   }
834
835   /**
836    * For {@code Map} types, returns the class type of the values of the {@code Map}.
837    *
838    * @return The value class type, or <jk>null</jk> if this class is not a Map.
839    */
840   @Override
841   public ClassMeta<?> getValueType() {
842      return keyValueTypes.get().valueType();
843   }
844
845   /**
846    * Returns the type parameters for this class as a {@link Type} array suitable for passing to
847    * {@link Converter#to(Object, Type, Type...)}.
848    *
849    * <p>
850    * The returned array depends on the kind of type:
851    * <ul>
852    *    <li>{@link Map} types: {@code [keyType, valueType]}
853    *    <li>{@link Collection} and {@link Optional} types: {@code [elementType, elementType.getParameters()...]}
854    *       — the element raw class followed by the element's own parameters, recursively flattened by one level.
855    *    <li>All other types: empty array.
856    * </ul>
857    *
858    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
859    * <ul>
860    *    <li><c>ClassMeta&lt;Optional&lt;Integer&gt;&gt;</c> → <c>[Integer]</c>
861    *    <li><c>ClassMeta&lt;List&lt;Integer&gt;&gt;</c> → <c>[Integer]</c>
862    *    <li><c>ClassMeta&lt;Map&lt;String,Integer&gt;&gt;</c> → <c>[String, Integer]</c>
863    *    <li><c>ClassMeta&lt;List&lt;Map&lt;String,Integer&gt;&gt;&gt;</c> → <c>[Map, String, Integer]</c>
864    * </ul>
865    *
866    * @return The type parameters as a {@link Type} array, never <jk>null</jk>.
867    */
868   public Type[] getParameters() { return parameters.get(); }
869
870   private Type[] findParameters() {
871      if (isMap()) {
872         var kt = getKeyType();
873         var vt = getValueType();
874         if (kt == null || vt == null)
875            return new Type[0];
876         return new Type[]{kt.inner(), vt.inner()};
877      }
878      if (isCollection() || isOptional()) {
879         var et = getElementType();
880         if (et == null)
881            return new Type[0];
882         var etParams = et.getParameters();
883         var result = new Type[1 + etParams.length];
884         result[0] = et.inner();
885         System.arraycopy(etParams, 0, result, 1, etParams.length);
886         return result;
887      }
888      return new Type[0];
889   }
890
891   /**
892    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class has a transform associated with it that allows it to be created from an InputStream.
893    *
894    * <p>
895    * Only returns <jk>true</jk> for types that can be explicitly constructed from an {@link InputStream} (e.g. via a
896    * public constructor or static factory). Excludes assignable supertypes and the generic {@code toString()} fallback.
897    *
898    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class has a transform associated with it that allows it to be created from an InputStream.
899    */
900   public boolean hasInputStreamMutater() {
901      // Exclude String: BasicConverter maps InputStream→String via stream.toString() which doesn't read content.
902      // Exclude supertypes of InputStream (e.g. Object, Closeable): assignability, not an explicit transform.
903      if (inner() == String.class || inner().isAssignableFrom(InputStream.class))
904         return false;
905      return BasicConverter.INSTANCE.canConvert(InputStream.class, inner());
906   }
907
908   /**
909    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class can be instantiated from the specified type via an explicit conversion.
910    *
911    * <p>
912    * Same-type conversions (identity) always return <jk>true</jk>. Strict supertype relationships (where this
913    * class is already a supertype of the source) return <jk>false</jk> — those are assignability, not explicit
914    * transforms.
915    *
916    * @param c The class type to convert from.
917    * @return <jk>true</jk> if an explicit conversion from {@code c} to this type exists.
918    */
919   public boolean hasMutaterFrom(Class<?> c) {
920      // Same type is always "convertible" (identity).
921      if (inner() == c)
922         return true;
923      // Exclude strict supertype: if this type is already a strict supertype of c, it's assignability only.
924      if (inner().isAssignableFrom(c))
925         return false;
926      // Exclude array→array: MarshallingSession handles those directly; BasicConverter infers
927      // array conversions from element types, which can produce false positives here.
928      if (inner().isArray() && c.isArray())
929         return false;
930      // Exclude Collections and Maps — MarshallingSession handles collection/map conversion directly.
931      // BasicConverter's generic conversions produce false positives that Mutaters never did.
932      if (Collection.class.isAssignableFrom(inner()) || Map.class.isAssignableFrom(inner()))
933         return false;
934      return BasicConverter.INSTANCE.canConvert(c, inner());
935   }
936
937   /**
938    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class can be instantiated from the specified type via an explicit conversion.
939    *
940    * @param c The class type to convert from.
941    * @return <jk>true</jk> if an explicit conversion from {@code c} to this type exists.
942    */
943   public boolean hasMutaterFrom(ClassMeta<?> c) {
944      return hasMutaterFrom(c.inner());
945   }
946
947   /**
948    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class can be transformed to the specified type via an explicit conversion.
949    *
950    * <p>
951    * Same-type conversions (identity) always return <jk>true</jk>. Cases where the target is already a strict
952    * supertype of this class return <jk>false</jk> — those are assignability, not explicit transforms.
953    *
954    * @param c The class type to convert to.
955    * @return <jk>true</jk> if an explicit conversion from this type to {@code c} exists.
956    */
957   public boolean hasMutaterTo(Class<?> c) {
958      // Same type is always "convertible" (identity).
959      if (inner() == c)
960         return true;
961      // Exclude strict supertype: if the target is already a strict supertype of this type, it's assignability only.
962      if (c.isAssignableFrom(inner()))
963         return false;
964      // Exclude array→array: MarshallingSession handles those directly; BasicConverter infers
965      // array conversions from element types, which can produce false positives here.
966      if (inner().isArray() && c.isArray())
967         return false;
968      // Exclude Collection/Map targets — MarshallingSession handles collection/map conversion directly.
969      // BasicConverter's generic conversions produce false positives that Mutaters never did.
970      if (Collection.class.isAssignableFrom(c) || Map.class.isAssignableFrom(c))
971         return false;
972      return BasicConverter.INSTANCE.canConvert(inner(), c);
973   }
974
975   /**
976    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class can be transformed to the specified type via an explicit conversion.
977    *
978    * @param c The class type to convert to.
979    * @return <jk>true</jk> if an explicit conversion from this type to {@code c} exists.
980    */
981   public boolean hasMutaterTo(ClassMeta<?> c) {
982      return hasMutaterTo(c.inner());
983   }
984
985   /**
986    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class has a transform associated with it that allows it to be created from a Reader.
987    *
988    * <p>
989    * Only returns <jk>true</jk> for types that can be explicitly constructed from a {@link Reader} (e.g. via a
990    * public constructor or static factory). Excludes assignable supertypes and the generic {@code toString()} fallback.
991    *
992    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class has a transform associated with it that allows it to be created from a Reader.
993    */
994   public boolean hasReaderMutater() {
995      // Exclude String: BasicConverter maps Reader→String via reader.toString() which doesn't read content.
996      // Exclude supertypes of Reader (e.g. Object, Closeable): assignability, not an explicit transform.
997      if (inner() == String.class || inner().isAssignableFrom(Reader.class))
998         return false;
999      return BasicConverter.INSTANCE.canConvert(Reader.class, inner());
1000   }
1001
1002   /**
1003    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class has a transform associated with it that allows it to be created from a String.
1004    *
1005    * <p>
1006    * Delegates to {@link #hasMutaterFrom(Class)} with {@link String} as the source type, which applies the
1007    * same exclusions: supertypes of {@link String} (e.g. {@link Object}), arrays, and {@link Collection}/{@link Map}
1008    * targets are excluded because those are handled by parsers or {@code MarshallingSession}, not explicit string transforms.
1009    *
1010    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class has a transform associated with it that allows it to be created from a String.
1011    */
1012   public boolean hasStringMutater() {
1013      return hasMutaterFrom(String.class);
1014   }
1015
1016   /**
1017    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this metadata represents an array of argument types.
1018    *
1019    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this metadata represents an array of argument types.
1020    */
1021   public boolean isArgs() { return cat.is(ARGS); }
1022
1023   /**
1024    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a bean.
1025    *
1026    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a bean.
1027    */
1028   @Override
1029   public boolean isBean() { return nn(getBeanMeta()); }
1030
1031   /**
1032    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link BeanMap}.
1033    *
1034    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link BeanMap}.
1035    */
1036   public boolean isBeanMap() { return cat.is(BEANMAP); }
1037
1038   /**
1039    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Boolean}.
1040    *
1041    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Boolean}.
1042    */
1043   @Override
1044   public boolean isBoolean() { return isAny(boolean.class, Boolean.class); }
1045
1046   /**
1047    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>.
1048    *
1049    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>.
1050    */
1051   public boolean isByteArray() { return is(byte[].class); }
1052
1053   /**
1054    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Calendar}.
1055    *
1056    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Calendar}.
1057    */
1058   public boolean isCalendar() { return cat.is(CALENDAR); }
1059
1060   /**
1061    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Character}.
1062    *
1063    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Character}.
1064    */
1065   public boolean isChar() { return isAny(char.class, Character.class); }
1066
1067   /**
1068    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link CharSequence}.
1069    *
1070    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link CharSequence}.
1071    */
1072   @Override
1073   public boolean isCharSequence() { return cat.is(CHARSEQ); }
1074
1075   /**
1076    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Collection}.
1077    *
1078    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Collection}.
1079    */
1080   @Override
1081   public boolean isCollection() { return cat.is(COLLECTION); }
1082
1083   /**
1084    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Collection} or is an array or {@link Optional}.
1085    *
1086    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Collection} or is an array or {@link Optional}.
1087    */
1088   public boolean isCollectionOrArrayOrOptional() { return cat.is(ARRAY) || is(Optional.class) || cat.is(COLLECTION); }
1089
1090   /**
1091    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Date}.
1092    *
1093    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Date}.
1094    */
1095   public boolean isDate() { return cat.is(DATE); }
1096
1097   /**
1098    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Date} or {@link Calendar}.
1099    *
1100    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Date} or {@link Calendar}.
1101    */
1102   @Override
1103   public boolean isDateOrCalendar() { return cat.is(DATE) || cat.is(CALENDAR); }
1104
1105   /**
1106    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Date} or {@link Calendar} or {@link Temporal}.
1107    *
1108    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Date} or {@link Calendar} or {@link Temporal}.
1109    */
1110   public boolean isDateOrCalendarOrTemporal() { return cat.is(DATE) || cat.is(CALENDAR) || cat.is(TEMPORAL) || cat.is(PERIOD); }
1111
1112   /**
1113    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Float} or {@link Double}.
1114    *
1115    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Float} or {@link Double}.
1116    */
1117   @Override
1118   public boolean isDecimal() { return cat.is(DECIMAL); }
1119
1120   /**
1121    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class implements {@link Delegate}, meaning it's a representation of some other
1122    * object.
1123    *
1124    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class implements {@link Delegate}.
1125    */
1126   public boolean isDelegate() { return cat.is(DELEGATE); }
1127
1128   /**
1129    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Double} or <jk>double</jk>.
1130    *
1131    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Double} or <jk>double</jk>.
1132    */
1133   @Override
1134   public boolean isDouble() { return isAny(Double.class, double.class); }
1135
1136   /**
1137    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Float} or <jk>float</jk>.
1138    *
1139    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Float} or <jk>float</jk>.
1140    */
1141   public boolean isFloat() { return isAny(Float.class, float.class); }
1142
1143   /**
1144    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is an {@link InputStream}.
1145    *
1146    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is an {@link InputStream}.
1147    */
1148   public boolean isInputStream() { return cat.is(INPUTSTREAM); }
1149
1150   /**
1151    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Integer} or <jk>int</jk>.
1152    *
1153    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Integer} or <jk>int</jk>.
1154    */
1155   public boolean isInteger() { return isAny(Integer.class, int.class); }
1156
1157   /**
1158    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class implements {@link Iterable} but is not a {@link Collection}.
1159    *
1160    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class implements {@link Iterable} but is not a {@link Collection}.
1161    * @since 9.2.1
1162    */
1163   public boolean isIterable() { return cat.is(ITERABLE); }
1164
1165   /**
1166    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class implements {@link Iterator} or {@link Enumeration}.
1167    *
1168    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class implements {@link Iterator} or {@link Enumeration}.
1169    * @since 9.2.1
1170    */
1171   public boolean isIterator() { return cat.is(ITERATOR); }
1172
1173   /**
1174    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is an {@link Iterator}, {@link Iterable} (non-Collection), or {@link BaseStream}.
1175    *
1176    * <p>
1177    * These types represent lazily-evaluated sequences that can be serialized as arrays.
1178    *
1179    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a streamable type.
1180    * @since 9.2.1
1181    */
1182   public boolean isStreamable() { return cat.is(ITERATOR) || cat.is(ITERABLE) || cat.is(STREAM); }
1183
1184   /**
1185    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class extends from {@link List}.
1186    *
1187    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class extends from {@link List}.
1188    */
1189   public boolean isList() { return cat.is(LIST); }
1190
1191   /**
1192    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Long} or <jk>long</jk>.
1193    *
1194    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Long} or <jk>long</jk>.
1195    */
1196   @Override
1197   public boolean isLong() { return isAny(Long.class, long.class); }
1198
1199   /**
1200    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Map}.
1201    *
1202    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Map}.
1203    */
1204   @Override
1205   public boolean isMap() { return cat.is(MAP); }
1206
1207   /**
1208    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Map} or it's a bean.
1209    *
1210    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Map} or it's a bean.
1211    */
1212   public boolean isMapOrBean() { return cat.is(MAP) || nn(getBeanMeta()); }
1213
1214   /**
1215    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Method}.
1216    *
1217    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is {@link Method}.
1218    */
1219   public boolean isMethod() { return is(Method.class); }

1221   /**
1222    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if instance of this object can be <jk>null</jk>.
1223    *
1224    * <p>
1225    * Objects can be <jk>null</jk>, but primitives cannot, except for chars which can be represented by
1226    * <code>(<jk>char</jk>)0</code>.
1227    *
1228    * @return <jk>true</jk> if instance of this class can be null.
1229    */
1230   public boolean isNullable() {
1231      if (isPrimitive())
1232         return is(char.class);
1233      return true;
1234   }
1235
1236   /**
1237    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Number}.
1238    *
1239    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link Number}.
1240    */
1241   @Override
1242   public boolean isNumber() { return cat.is(NUMBER); }
1243
1244   /**
1245    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Reader}.
1246    *
1247    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Reader}.
1248    */
1249   public boolean isReader() { return cat.is(READER); }

1251   /**
1252    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class extends from {@link Set}.

1254    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class extends from {@link Set}.
1255    */
1256   public boolean isSet() { return cat.is(SET); }

1258   /**
1259    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Short} or <jk>short</jk>.
1260    *
1261    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is either {@link Short} or <jk>short</jk>.
1262    */
1263   public boolean isShort() { return isAny(Short.class, short.class); }

1265   /**
1266    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link BaseStream} (includes {@link Stream}).
1267    *
1268    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a subclass of {@link BaseStream}.
1269    * @since 9.2.1
1270    */
1271   public boolean isStream() { return cat.is(STREAM); }

1273   /**
1274    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link String}.
1275    *
1276    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link String}.
1277    */
1278   public boolean isString() { return is(String.class); }

1281    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this class is a {@link Temporal}.

1285   @Override
1286   public boolean isTemporal() { return cat.is(TEMPORAL); }

1293   public boolean isDuration() { return cat.is(DURATION); }

1300   public boolean isPeriod() { return cat.is(PERIOD); }

1307   @Override
